recommendations.title: Greater
recommendations.overview: The incredible true story of the greatest walk-on in the history of college football.

recommendations.title: Flash of Genius
recommendations.overview: In this David vs. Goliath drama based on a true story, college professor Robert Kearns goes up against the giants of the auto industry when they fail to give him credit for inventing intermittent windshield wipers. Kearns doggedly pursues recognition

recommendations.title: Breathe
recommendations.overview: Based on the true story of Robin, a handsome, brilliant and adventurous man whose life takes a dramatic turn when polio leaves him paralyzed.

recommendations.title: Walk. Ride. Rodeo.
recommendations.overview: Tells the incredible true story of Amberley Snyder, a nationally ranked rodeo barrel racer who defies the odds after barely surviving a car accident that leaves her paralyzed from the waist down.

recommendations.title: Pelé: Birth of a Legend
recommendations.overview: The life story of Brazilian football legend, Pele.

recommendations.title: The Blind Side
recommendations.overview: The story of Michael Oher, a homeless and traumatized boy who became an All American football player and first round NFL draft pick with the help of a caring woman and her family.

recommendations.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
recommendations.overview: Powered with incredible speed, Sonic The Hedgehog embraces his new home on Earth. That is, until Sonic sparks the attention of super-uncool evil genius Dr. Robotnik. Now it’s super-villain vs. super-sonic in an all-out race across the globe to stop R

recommendations.title: Invincible

recommendations.title: The Imitation Game
recommendations.overview: Based on the real life story of legendary cryptanalyst Alan Turing, the film portrays the nail-biting race against time by Turing and his brilliant team of code-breakers at Britain's top-secret Government Code and Cypher School at Bletchley Park, dur

recommendations.title: Jerry Seinfeld: 23 Hours to Kill
recommendations.overview: Jerry Seinfeld takes the stage in New York and tackles talking vs. texting, bad buffets vs. so-called "great" restaurants and the magic of Pop Tarts.
